'''Pegg''' was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="GH" /> located in the [[Pegg system/Legends|Pegg system]],<ref name="EAOC" /> a part of the [[Tynquay sector/Legends|Tynquay sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It hosted multiple [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]], with the concourse of the main one being the site of the store named [[Merrk's Weapons Shop]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], several members of the [[Yesgar's pirates|group]] of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] led by [[Yesgar]], who had a hideout on<ref name="GH" /> the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]]<ref name="EAOC Antared">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Antared system/Legends|Antared system]]</ref> celestial body [[Antared III]], were observed entering the shop, after which they never registered their purchases with Pegg's [[Pegg government|local government]].<ref name="GH" />

==Behind the scenes==
Pegg was introduced in ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Handbook]]'', a [[1993]] sourcebook published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the game, the celestial body was mentioned in a hypothetical example in a section advising gamemaster players on the use of various paper handouts in their games.<ref name="GH" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' later placed the Pegg [[Star system/Legends|system]] in grid square R-4. The book's co-author<ref name="TEA" /> [[Jason Fry]] has [[:File:JasonFryAtlas.png|stated]] that the [[Star/Legends|star]] systems listed in the Appendix section of ''The Essential Atlas'' are named after the most prominent [[Planet/Legends|planets]] or celestial bodies located in those systems. This article therefore considers the existence of Pegg to be [[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="Fry">{{Twitter|jasoncfry|status/294887358405410816|[[Jason Fry]]|quote=Pretty much." {{C|In response to "the systems in the Atlas's appendix are named after their most prominent planet/celestial body, correct?"}} and "planets not assigned a system in [a] published source can be assumed to reside in [the] system of [the] same name.|archivefile=File:JasonFryAtlas.png|archiveurl=https://archive.is/zThQV}}</ref>
